subject: Discrete Random Variables
topic: §14 Probability Mass Function and Cumulative Distribution Function
question: Consider the experiment of rolling a fair die twice. Let $X(i,j) = \max\{i, j\}$. Find the equation of $\Pr(x)$.
explanation: The pmf of $X$ is $p(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{2x-1}{36} & \text{if } x = 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases} = \frac{2x-1}{36} I_{\{1,2,3,4,5,6\}}(x)$ where $I_{\{1,2,3,4,5,6\}}(x) = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if } x \in \{1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6\} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$. In general, we define the indicator function of a set $A$ to be the function $I_A(x) = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if } x \in A \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$. Note that if the range of a random variable is $\Omega = \{x_1, x_2, \ldots\}$ then $p(x) \ge 0$, $x \in \Omega$ and $\sum_{x \in \Omega} p(x) = 1$. All random variables (discrete, continuous or mixed) have a distribution function or a cumulative distribution function, abbreviated cdf. It is a function giving the probability that the random variable $X$ is less than or equal to $x$, for every value $x$. For a discrete random variable, the cumulative distribution function is found by summing up the probabilities. That is, $F(a) = \Pr(X \le a) = \sum_{x \le a} p(x)$.
